ebirdst — project instructions for Claude

Source: CLAUDE.md

This file is local-only (gitignored) and layers on top of the global R style guide in ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md. Follow both; this file adds project-specific workflow requirements.

Formatting and linting — always run these

  • After writing or editing any file in R/, run air format R/. The repo’s air.toml scopes formatting to R/ only (data-raw/, examples/, tests/, and makefile.R are intentionally excluded), so air format . is also safe to run from the repo root.
  • After writing or editing any file in R/, run jarl check R/ (or jarl check . — jarl.toml restricts it to R/ regardless). Fix obvious/auto-fixable issues with jarl check --fix R/. For warnings that require judgment (e.g. internal_function on a ::: call with no public alternative), use judgment rather than blindly forcing a fix.
  • Do this on every change, not just when explicitly asked to format or lint.
  • Never run air/jarl over tests/, data-raw/, examples/, or makefile.R — those are intentionally out of scope per air.toml / jarl.toml.

Tests

  • Every new exported or internal function needs an accompanying test in tests/testthat/test-{name}.R (see the global CLAUDE.md for naming and structure conventions). Don’t skip this because a change feels small.
  • When modifying an existing function’s behavior, update or extend its existing tests rather than leaving them stale.
  • Run the affected test file(s) with devtools::test_file() before running the full suite; run devtools::test() before considering a change done.
  • Use the "yebsap-example" example dataset for integration tests — it’s already downloaded by tests/testthat/setup.R. Don’t add tests that require downloading real species data.

Documentation

  • After changing any roxygen2 comment, re-run devtools::document() (this regenerates NAMESPACE and man/*.Rd). Never hand-edit NAMESPACE or files under man/.
  • If a function’s @export tag is missing or misplaced, that’s a real bug (it silently breaks the public API) — not a style nitpick.

Package development workflow

  • Bump the version in DESCRIPTION and add a bullet to NEWS.md for any user-facing change (new function, changed argument, bug fix affecting output).
  • Prefer devtools::load_all() over library(ebirdst)/install.packages() when iterating locally.
  • Before considering larger changes complete, run devtools::check() and resolve new NOTEs/WARNINGs/ERRORs it introduces (see makefile.R for the fuller release checklist — vignettes, pkgdown site, win-builder checks — which is only needed at release time, not for routine changes).
  • Vignettes only execute locally (BUILD_VIGNETTES=TRUE), never during R CMD check — don’t try to make them run in CI.

Git and GitHub

  • This repo is typically contributed to via fork + upstream remote (see CONTRIBUTING.md): changes land on a branch, then a PR against ebird/ebirdst.
  • You have permission to run git and gh (including gh pr create) directly. Still follow the general git safety protocol: create new commits rather than amending, never force-push main, never skip hooks unless explicitly asked, and confirm before anything destructive (reset --hard, force-push, branch deletion) even though the command itself doesn’t require a prompt.

On this page

Developed by Matthew Strimas-Mackey, Shawn Ligocki, Tom Auer, Daniel Fink.
